- The ketone C=O (where O is mapped as :10) gets protonated by the sulfonic acid
- The sulfonic acid loses its proton to the ketone oxygen
- The sulfonate becomes the conjugate base

This is an acid-catalyzed enolization / protonation of a ketone by p-toluenesulfonic acid.

- Formal charge change on O:21: neutral → negative (O-)

The reaction center is the proton transfer from TsOH to the ketone oxygen.

In terms of a compact reaction edit summary:
- Break O:21-H:20 bond
- Form O:10-H:20 bond
- O:10 gains positive charge (protonation)
- O:21 gains negative charge (deprotonation)

This is a tert-butyl ester of an α,β-unsaturated acid. The C22=C21-C20 is the enone portion (actually it's an enoate: C22(=O23)-CH21=CH20).

Reactant 4: CC(C)(C)O[C:11](=[CH:10]C1CCCC1)[O-:12]

Product: CC(C)(C)O[C:22](=[CH:21][CH:20](CCCCI)[CH:10](C1CCCC1)[C:11](=[O:12])OC(C)(C)C)[O-:23]

- CH:10 is connected to C1CCCC1 and C:11

This looks like a Michael addition / conjugate addition reaction where:

- After addition, C20 gets a new bond to CH10

- C22=O23 becomes C22-O-23 (the ester becomes an enolate)
- C11=CH10 becomes C11=O12 (the enolate becomes a ketone/ester)

C:22(=CH:21)(O-:23) — this is now an enolate
CH:21—CH:20(CCCCI)—CH:10(C1CCCC1)—C:11(=O:12)(OC(C)(C)C)

3. Bond change: C:22=O:23 → C:22-O-:23 (ester carbonyl becomes enolate)
4. Bond change: C:11=CH:10 → C:11=O:12 (enolate becomes ester carbonyl)

Actually wait - let me reconsider. The C:11 in reactant is bonded to =CH:10 and O-:12. In product, C:11 is bonded to =O:12 and OC(C)(C)C. So C:11 went from being an enolate carbon (C11=CH10, O-12) to being a ketone/ester carbon (C11=O12).

And C:22 went from being an ester carbonyl (C22=O23) to being an enolate (C22=CH21, O-23).

The reaction center atoms are: C:22, O:23, C:21, C:20, C:10, C:11, O:12

The bond changes:
- C21=C20 → C21-C20 (double to single)
- C20-C10 new bond formed
- C22=O23 → C22-O23 (double to single, O becomes anionic)
- C11=CH10 → C11-O12 (the enolate C=CH becomes C=O, and O-12 changes from anionic oxygen to carbonyl oxygen)

Wait, I need to be more precise. Let me re-read the SMIRKS.

Reactant fragment for the enoate: CC(C)(C)O[C:22](=[O:23])[CH:21]=[CH:20]CCCCI
- C22 has double bond to O23, single bond to CH21

Reactant fragment for the enolate: CC(C)(C)O[C:11](=[CH:10]C1CCCC1)[O-:12]

Product fragment: CC(C)(C)O[C:22](=[CH:21][CH:20](CCCCI)[CH:10](C1CCCC1)[C:11](=[O:12])OC(C)(C)C)[O-:23]

So the bond changes at the reaction center:

2. C21=C20 (double) → C21-C20 (single) — bond order decrease
